message:
fix(ft-nn): validate Linear in_features and Dropout probability to fail closed on invalid inputs

Two safety hardening changes to the neural network module layer:

1. Linear::new() now rejects in_features == 0 with a structured
   DispatchError. Previously, constructing a Linear layer with zero
   input features would compute bound = 1/sqrt(0) = Inf, generating
   weight tensors filled with Inf/NaN values that would silently
   propagate through the forward pass and produce garbage. The check
   ensures the error surfaces immediately at layer construction time
   rather than during training.

2. Dropout::forward() now validates that the dropout probability p is
   finite and within [0, 1] before executing. Previously, NaN or
   out-of-range probabilities would flow into the random mask generation
   and produce undefined behavior (e.g., NaN comparisons always evaluate
   to false, effectively disabling dropout without any warning). The
   check returns a structured DispatchError so the caller gets a clear
   diagnostic.

Both changes follow the project's "fail closed" safety invariant:
invalid hyperparameters are rejected at the API boundary rather than
silently producing incorrect results.

Also adds ft-dispatch as a direct dependency in Cargo.toml since the
validation errors use DispatchError and DispatchKeyError types.

Tests added:
- dropout_invalid_probability_fails_closed (NaN, -0.1, 1.1)
- linear_rejects_zero_in_features

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
